title: Robust Shaggy Norwegian Lamb Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful one-pot Norwegian dish featuring lamb, dark rum, and citrus, made with ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly ingredients and a shaggy texture from kneaded dough, perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: In a large pot, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Add the lamb cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the dark rum, orange juice, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
In a separate bowl, combine the flour and water to form a shaggy dough, then knead for 5 minutes.
Add the dough to the pot, stirring to combine with the lamb and sauce.
Add the sliced carrots to the pot, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1.5 hours.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
